concerns.text: Baseline proportion (0.05) for FR-005a binomial test justified by 'John et al. (2022)' but research.md states 'NO verified source found'. Statistical justification for baseline relies on unverified citation. FR-005b sensitivity analysis mitigates but baseline choice should have verified empirical support or be explicitly policy-based.

Bias-adjusted rate uses 'domain-weighted averaging' but weighting method (inverse-variance, equal, population-weighted) is unspecified. This affects reproducibility and statistical interpretation of the adjusted rate.
Baseline handling heuristic (average of variant rates) is acknowledged as reduced rigor but should explicitly state this affects effect size reconstruction uncertainty, not just flag in notes.
